title: aws-kms: MessageType::Digest removes 4096-byte limit — preserve this
description: AwsKmsBackend::sign() pre-hashes with SHA-256 and sends MessageType::Digest (secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:154-167). MessageType::Raw hashes internally but imposes a 4096-byte limit; MessageType::Digest has no size limit and is semantically identical (both algorithms sign the SHA-256 hash). A contributor might remove the pre-hashing step thinking it's redundant. It isn't — MessageType::Raw would silently break for messages > 4096 bytes.
